title: Crunchy Jammy Ethiopian Carrots Dulet
description: Traditional Ethiopian dish made with carrots, onions, and spices. A popular breakfast or snack item, known for its crunchy texture and sweet flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine grated carrots, onions, garlic, ginger, fenugreek seeds, ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Heat clarified butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the carrot mixture to the skillet and stir-fry for 5-7 minutes, until the carrots start to soften.
Dust the mixture with all-purpose flour and stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes, until the mixture thickens.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small, cylindrical shapes, about 8-10 cm long and 2.5 cm in diameter.
Dip each cylindrical shape into a beaten egg and then roll in all-purpose flour to coat.
Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat and cook the dulet for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy.
Serve hot with a side of stews or salads, if desired.
Repeat with the remaining carrot mixture.
